The Powerball lottery on 11 May 2026 has drawn attention with a massive $57 million ultimate jackpot on the line, creating strong excitement among players across the United States. It is one of the most widely played lottery games, operated through the Multi-State Lottery Association (MUSL) and available only in participating US states. The game is simple but highly unpredictable, based on selecting numbers and hoping they match the official draw.The players choose five numbers between 1 and 69 and one extra Powerball number ranging between 1 and 26. Since each draw is random, it cannot be predicted in advance, and this unpredictability makes it intriguing to many.Different prize levels depend on how the winning combination comes out, and hence, there is always room to win in any lottery draw. The jackpot of $57 million makes this draw more interesting than ever before.Reportedly, the jackpot rose to $57 million with a cash value of $25.7 million ahead of the Monday, May 11.The winning numbers for the Monday, May 11, Powerball drawing areThe "Power Play" multiplier isThe Powerball lottery game is a game of choosing numbers and hoping that those numbers will match those chosen randomly in the draw process. The lottery is run by the lottery system in multiple jurisdictions, hence the game covers a broad geographical area and has huge jackpots.It is primarily a number-matching game. This involves choosing five main numbers out of a pool of numbers within the range of 1 to 69. These numbers are referred to as white balls. Thereafter, an extra ball number is chosen in the range of 1 to 26, referred to as Powerball.The process is normally done by a device to ensure randomness of the selection process. The whole process is entirely random. According to experts, the excitement lies in the uncertainty of the results.There is a strict schedule for drawing numbers for Powerball. It is drawn three times a week: onThe lottery numbers are drawnThe schedule remains constant throughout the year, creating a certain rhythm for those who play regularly. The participants are aware when the results are expected, although they might not play during each lottery draw.The ticket sales stop some hours before the actual lottery draw starts. However, the time of the cutoff can differ depending on the area. The lottery is usually closed for sales several hours before the draw in some states, while in other regions, the tickets can be purchased closer to the drawing hour. Despite the established schedule, the mood surrounding each lottery draw may change depending on the current jackpot size.Playing Powerball is designed to be straightforward so that anyone can participate without confusion.There is also an optional feature called Power Play. This costs an extra dollar and multiplies non-jackpot winnings. The multiplier is randomly selected before the draw and can significantly increase smaller prizes.Winning Powerball is based on the number of matches that the player gets from the official drawing. Partial matches would still yield smaller amounts, and thus many people are encouraged to continue checking their tickets despite not winning the jackpot.A match for the Powerball alone is enough to give you a small prize that would be fixed at around $4. One or two more matches might give you winnings that might be a little higher.A match of three numbers could give you a small prize, while four would yield an even higher reward. If five numbers are correctly matched without the Powerball, you are assured of a substantial payout worth about $1 million. To claim the jackpot, a player needs to have five numbers matched with the Powerball number.Powerball tickets are available in many everyday locations across the United States. These include gas stations, grocery stores, convenience shops, and even some airport terminals.The ticket price is fixed at $2. Players who choose the Power Play option pay $3 in total.This accessibility is part of what keeps the game active. It does not require planning or special access. It fits into regular routines, like buying groceries or fuel.The Powerball draws are made three times a week on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Saturdays at 10:59 pm Eastern Time. This remains consistent throughout the year, thus making it easier for players to note this down.In order to win the grand prize, players must match five white balls and one Powerball number. Smaller rewards are also offered based on partial matches, even if just one number is matched correctly.The Power Play option is an additional service offered by the game for a price of $1. In case of non-grand prize wins, they get multiplied by either 2x, 3x, 4x, 5x, and sometimes even 10x.The Powerball tickets are sold through authorised vendors, including various gas stations, convenience stores, supermarket stores, and even some of the airport stores in the USA.Yes, the players have two options: to choose the numbers manually or let the system randomly pick them from the lottery machine using the quick pick button.
Article Summary: The Powerball lottery on 11 May 2026 has drawn attention with a massive $57 million ultimate jackpot on the line. It is one of the most widely played lottery games, operated through the Multi-State Lottery Association (MUSL) and available only in participating US states. The game is simple but highly unpredictable, based on selecting numbers and hoping they match the official draw.

Article Title: “Three-against-one situation”: El Rubius opens up on being “targeted” in MrBeast’s viral $1M challenge
Article Text:
El Rubius reacted honestly after his elimination from MrBeast’s $1 million challenge, calling it “unfair” and claiming he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings. His response felt real and balanced, especially after MrBeast’s team later sent him a cake, softening the moment.

El Rubius reflects on a tough and “unfair” exit







El Rubius did not just walk away from MrBeast’s $1 million streamer challenge. He opened up. Soon after his elimination, the Spanish creator shared a raw reaction with fans, giving them a closer look at what the experience felt like from inside the game. It was not polished or carefully framed. It felt real, and that is exactly why it caught attention so quickly.The clip and his response have since fueled conversations across the streaming space. Some viewers believe intense competitions like this naturally lead to uneven situations. Others feel Rubius may have had a point about being singled out. Either way, his words have added a human layer to a challenge that many only saw as entertainment.Speaking through an Instagram Story, Rubius did not hide his frustration, but he also made sure to thank his supporters. He said, "Guys, I just got knocked out of the game. It was a bit of an unfair elimination, but hey, at least I managed to walk away with $50,000 for you all, which I’ve already distributed. It went to the mods, the subs, and the non-subs alike."He further added, "I’m happy, of course, though admittedly, it was incredibly tough.I wasn't really a fan of the challenge itself, either. But then again... it was basically a three-against-one situation the entire time. I mean, they were targeting me specifically the whole way through. I kept trying to go after RaKai, but, well, he actually just got eliminated in the end. So, at least justice was served for Abraham. Abraham, if you’re watching this, we were all rooting for you, and standing right there with you."His message carried mixed emotions. There was disappointment, yes, but also pride in how he handled things. Giving away his $50,000 winnings to his community stood out as a gesture that meant more than the result itself.He continued with another honest note, saying, "And in the end, karma finally caught up with that little punk. Sorry, I’m being a bit toxic right now, but let's just hope the MrBeast team doesn't see this! Guys, thank you all so much. Seriously, thank you for all the support and for just being there."He added, "Every time I asked for something in the chat or whatever it was, you guys were right there. You’re incredible. I’m a little disappointed in myself, personally, mostly for not performing better, but hey, I gave it my all, and we still walked away with $50,000, so we’ve got to look on the bright side. All right, guys, big hugs to all my brothers out there!"Interestingly, the story did not end with frustration. Later that day, Rubius revealed that MrBeast’s team had sent him a cake after his exit. It was a small gesture, but it shifted the mood. It showed that even in a high-pressure challenge, there is still room for respect.In the end, Rubius’ reaction felt honest and grounded. That is what stayed with people.
Article Summary: El Rubius was eliminated from MrBeast's $1 million streamer challenge. He called it "unfair" and said he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings.

Article Title: Ipl Ticket Scam: IPL craze costs techie Rs 1.46 lakh in fake RCB vs CSK ticket scam
Article Text:
Image credit: AI generated pic

Watch Devdutt Padikkal press conference: Chinnaswamy pitch, Tim David’s power & RCB’s batting strategy Devdutt Padikkal press conference: Chinnaswamy pitch, Tim David’s power & RCB’s batting strategy

RCB register their second win

Poll Do you think the demand for IPL tickets is too high every season? Yes, it's excessive. No, it's just right.

NEW DELHI: IPL comes and the demand for tickets and passes go soaring up by the fans. The desperation to get inside the stadium to watch their favourite teams in action in the IPL go high and high. When the tickets get sold out, the demand gets too hig, and the cricket fans even ask for tickets in black. But for a 25-year-old techie the craze for IPL tickets for RCB vs CSK turned too costly and he was duped for a massive Rs. 1.46 lakh.The techie, a resident of B Narayanapura, fell into a trap by a post posted by a fraudster who posed himself as a ticket seller on Instagram.As per a report in Deccan Herald, the techie alleged in a complaint to Mahadevapura Police that an individual claiming to be Sumit Biswal promised he could arrange IPL tickets along with food coupons.As per the techie, Biswal was introduced him as a senior supervisor for ticket counter at M Chinnaswamy Stadium"He asked me to come near the stadium gate number 10, saying someone would deliver the tickets within minutes, and even sent an email confirmation to gain my trust," the complainant told Deccan Herald."Initially, I agreed to buy two tickets for Rs 3,700 each,but he kept asking for more money under various pretexts such as refundable security deposits, additional ID cards and food coupons.Trusting him, I made multiple payments - even using my mother’s bank account after exhausting my own limits—and ended up transferring around Rs 1.46 lakh," he added.Bhuvneshwar Kumar’s incisive spell of 3-41, coming after a marauding batting show from Tim David, Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal, propelled Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) to a commanding 43-run victory over Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in their IPL 2026 encounter at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday.The contest saw RCB’s batting unit dismantle CSK’s attack with relentless hitting, as David’s 70 not out off just 25 balls and Patidar’s unbeaten 48 off only 19 balls provided for the late fireworks after Padikkal’s fluent 50 off 29 balls had set the tone for the daunting total.Their combined effort lifted RCB to a daunting 250/3, thus setting a new record for highest total in IPL 2026. In reply, CSK faltered under scoreboard pressure, with Sarfaraz Khan’s 50 off 24 balls and Prashant Veer’s 43 the lone acts of resistance amid a string of failures from the top order.Bhuvneshwar was at his disciplined best and even crossed the 200-mark in terms of wickets in IPL, with others also chipping in as CSK were bowled out for 207 in 19.4 overs. The comprehensive win also meant RCB have registered four consecutive triumphs over CSK for the first time in the IPL’s history.
Article Summary: Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) beat Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in their IPL 2026 encounter at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday. Tim David, Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal put on a marauding batting show to lift RCB to a daunting 250/3. RCB have registered four consecutive triumphs over CSK for the first time in the IPL's history.

Article Title: Clavicular Viral Controversy: "Trans women are.....": Clavicular’s viral moment with trans women sparks fresh conversation on internet culture
Article Text:
A viral clip featuring Clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Instead of conflict, the exchange highlights shared influence and mutual awareness. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration rather than ownership.

Clavicular and trans women share a surprising realization about online trends







The video of Clavicular, a short-video producer, is going around the Internet, yet not the reason why. There is no yelling or over-the-top drama. It is a mere, everyday talk that seems almost novel nowadays, in the age of internet, which is overflowing with content. The scene is easy-going, the tone of the narrative is straightforward, and the characters appear more concerned with conversation than combat.The message that is being addressed is the flow of the conversation. It discusses internet culture, the way people communicate with one another and the place of trends. Rather than bringing out a debate, the encounter towards curiosity. It quietly brings attention to something that a lot of people who use the internet notice but don't often say out loud. People in different communities often have a bigger impact on each other than they think.The conversation begins with a confident and lighthearted comment from one of the trans women present. She says, "You know that trans women are like the OG lip masters, right? We are very talented in the program. Like, I do the peptides, I'm on Zeta." Her tone carries a mix of pride and humor, setting a relaxed vibe at the table.Clavicular responds in a way that keeps the mood steady but adds a different angle.He says, "That is funny because you guys were using a lot of the words in like the South Korean subreddits in like 2014." It’s not confrontational. If anything, it feels like an observation. He points to how certain words and ideas may have traveled across different online spaces over time.Another voice joins in and brings the moment full circle. "Guys, we're like the OGs. I saw a couple of posts about that. We're like copying each other." That line hits in a simple but honest way. It reflects something familiar. Online culture is rarely created in isolation. It grows through sharing, borrowing, and sometimes even unknowingly repeating.That's what makes the clip so powerful. It doesn't want to make a point or win an argument. It only shows a group of people talking, listening, and figuring something out together.This moment feels different in a space where viral content often leads to fights. It reminds people that conversations can be calm, thoughtful, and still important. And sometimes, that honest silence is what makes it work.
Article Summary: A viral clip featuring Clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration.

Article Title: Toronto Maple Leafs fire head coach Craig Berube in another major roster shake-up following their disappointing run
Article Text:
Toronto Maple Leafs Coach Craig Berube has fired head coach Craig Berube on Wednesday, marking another major shake-up for the franchise (Image via Getty Images)

Craig Berube dismissed as Toronto Maple Leafs head coach , search for new coach begins

Watch Maple Leafs Fire HC Craig Berube! | Instant Analysis Maple Leafs Fire HC Craig Berube! | Instant Analysis

In a shocking decision, the Toronto Maple Leafs have fired head coach Craig Berube on Wednesday, marking another major shake-up for the franchise after a disappointing 2025-26 season. The organization confirmed that no replacement has been named yet as the search for a new bench boss begins under recently appointed general manager John Chayka.Speculations floated around his name after Brad Treliving was fired from the team. It is to be noted that the 60-year-old went 84-62-18 in two seasons after taking over for Sheldon Keefe, who was fired May 9, 2024. Following the firing decision, Toronto Maple Leafs general manager John Chayka stated that players were not consulted leading up to it.The Maple Leafs (32-36-14) ended the season 0-6-1, finishing last in the Atlantic Division and 15th in the Eastern Conference, and missed the Stanley Cup Playoffs for the first time since 2016-17.In a major roster shake-up, the Toronto Maple Leafs dismissed their head coach , Craig Berube, on Wednesday, after he had spent two seasons behind Toronto’s bench and posted an 84-62-18 record during his tenure. He had been hired in 2024 to replace Sheldon Keefe, arriving with strong credentials after leading the St.Louis Blues to their first-ever Stanley Cup title in 2019.Following the decision, Maple Leafs General Manager John Chayka stated, "Craig is a tremendous coach and an even better person. This decision is more reflective of an organizational shift and an opportunity for a fresh start than it is an evaluation of Craig. We are grateful for his leadership, professionalism, and commitment to the Maple Leafs organization and wish Craig and his family nothing but the best moving forward. "According to the NHL, the Toronto Maple Leafs (32-36-14) ended the season 0-6-1, finishing last in the Atlantic Division and 15th in the Eastern Conference, and missed the Stanley Cup Playoffs for the first time since 2016-17. Last season, they won the Atlantic with 108 points (52-26-4).They defeated the Ottawa Senators in six games in the Eastern Conference First Round before losing to the eventual Stanley Cup champion Florida Panthers in seven games in the second round. Toronto has advanced past the opening round of the playoffs twice since 2004 and has not won a Stanley Cup championship since 1967.As they are now busy hiring for another head coach, John Chayka was asked whether prior coaching experience in the National Hockey League would be an important requirement when hiring the next coach for the Toronto Maple Leafs.He added, “Experience in the NHL in larger markets could be an asset, and will be weighed, but I wouldn’t discount anything at this time. … I think regardless of who the coach is, we should have an organizational vision on how we play and the things we value, and then certainly through that process align on it.”Chayka also emphasized that regardless of who the coach is, the team should have an organizational vision on how we play and the things we value, and then certainly, through that process, align on it.Toronto now enters the offseason with the No. 1 overall pick in the upcoming draft and a new leadership group tasked with restoring the team to contention. Moreover, Berube's replacement will likely define the next phase of the Maple Leafs’ long-term rebuild.
Article Summary: The 60-year-old went 84-62-18 in two seasons after taking over for Sheldon Keefe, who was fired May 9, 2024. The Maple Leafs (32-36-14) ended the season 0-6-1, finishing last in the Atlantic Division and 15th in the Eastern Conference. Toronto has advanced past the opening round of the playoffs twice since 2004 and has not won a Stanley Cup championship since 1967.

Article Title: Mumbai autorickshaw drivers get water bottles
Article Text: MUMBAI: An initiative called "Who is Hussain (Mumbai)" has started helping the city's "silent warriors'" the rickshaw drivers with something they badly needed in the mercilessly hot weather.The team has just distributed 500 vacuum insulated steel bottles to autorickshaw drivers across the city, helping them stay hydrated while encouraging a shift away from single-use plastic bottles."Autorickshaw drivers work tirelessly in extreme weather conditions to keep the city moving. Through this initiative, we wanted not only to support them with something useful during the harsh summer, but also promote the use of reusable steel bottles instead of plastic, which is harmful to our environment," said Ali Akbar Razvi of "Who is Hussain Mumbai."Razvi added that the initiative was met with appreciation and smiles from drivers across various locations, making the campaign a "memorable and meaningful experience for the volunteers as well."
Article Summary: An initiative called "Who is Hussain (Mumbai)" has started helping the city's "silent warriors'" The team has just distributed 500 vacuum insulated steel bottles to autorickshaw drivers across the city. The initiative was met with appreciation and smiles from drivers across various locations.

Article Title: Ben Shelton and Trinity Rodman Combined Net Worth: Inside tennis star and US soccer team forward's earnings, endorsements income and rising wealth

Ben Shelton and Trinity Rodman (via Trinity Rodman / Instagram)

Ben Shelton and Trinity Rodman’s combined earnings and growing wealth

American tennis star Ben Shelton and U.S. soccer forward Trinity Rodman have become one of the most talked-about young sports couples in recent years. Article Title: Madan Dilawar: ‘No big deal’: Raj edu min on NEET-UG 2026 cancellation
Article Text: JAIPUR: Rajasthan school education minister Madan Dilawar, Tuesday, said the cancellation of the NEET-UG 2026 examination was “no big deal”, asserting that authorities took the decision after finding irregularities in the national-level exam attended by nearly 23 lakh students.“Investigating agencies must have found that something has gone wrong with the exam and decided to cancel it. There is no big deal in this. Wherever malpractices come to light, govt will take action to fix it,” Dilawar said in a widely circulated video in which he spoke to the media in Jodhpur.The minister also warned of strict action against all those involved in the alleged paper leak.“Whether the paper leak is connected to Sikar, West Bengal or even Kashmir, whoever is at fault, wherever they may be, will not be spared,” he said.Following criticism over the remarks, the minister’s media team, Wednesday clarified that the viral video did not contain his complete statement.“The minister had said that if there are fears that the paper had been leaked then cancelling it is the right thing to do keeping in mind the future of so many students. In a larger context, this step is important,” the media team stated.Dilawar’s remarks came amid allegations by Congress that the BJP govt attempted to suppress details related to the case to avoid embarrassment.
Article Summary: Rajasthan school education minister Madan Dilawar, Tuesday, said the cancellation of the NEET-UG 2026 examination was “no big deal” He said authorities took the decision after finding irregularities in the national-level exam attended by nearly 23 lakh students.

Article Title: Rhode Island: 6 lakes larger than Rhode Island in the US: Lake Huron and Lake Michigan are nearly 15 times larger
Article Text:
PC: National Museum of Great Lakes

Watch ‘Good Luck With Aliens’: Pilot-ATC Conversation After 'UFO'-Aircraft Encounter Over Rhode Island ‘Good Luck With Aliens’: Pilot-ATC Conversation After 'UFO'-Aircraft Encounter Over Rhode Island

US lakes larger than Rhode Island

Lake of the Woods

Lake Ontario

Lake Erie

PC: Lake Erie Living

Lake Michigan

PC: WorldAtlas

Lake Huron

Lake Superior

PC: Center for great lakes Literacy







Rhode Island may be the smallest state in the United States by area, but comparing it with some of North America’s largest lakes makes it seem even tinier. The state covers just 1,214 square miles, which is surprisingly small once you realise that six freshwater lakes in or partly connected to the US are actually larger. Some of these lakes are so vast that they behave more like inland seas, with rough waves, changing weather, and shorelines that stretch far beyond the horizon. Five belong to the Great Lakes system, while the sixth sits along the US-Canada border and contains thousands of islands. Together, these lakes completely change how people think about freshwater geography in North America. Their size, wildlife, and economic importance make them some of the most remarkable natural features on the continent.According to WorldAtlas reports, 6 freshwater lakes in or partly connected to the United States are larger than the entire state of Rhode Island.Lake of the Woods covers around 1,679 square miles, making it comfortably larger than Rhode Island. The lake stretches across northern Minnesota and into Canada, reportedly containing more than 14,500 islands. Experts say that is more islands than any other lake in the world.The lake has become famous for fishing, especially walleye fishing, and attracts visitors throughout the year. Wildlife around the shoreline includes pelicans, herons, mallards, and black bears. On calm days, parts of the lake can appear almost ocean-like because of its huge open water areas.At roughly 7,340 square miles, Lake Ontario is the smallest Great Lake by surface area, though it still dwarfs Rhode Island many times over. The lake forms part of the border between New York and the Canadian province of Ontario.Lake Ontario plays a major role in shipping because it connects the Great Lakes system to the Atlantic Ocean through the St Lawrence Seaway. Around 9 million people reportedly rely on the lake for drinking water. The Niagara River also feeds into it, linking the lake closely with Niagara Falls.Lake Erie spans around 9,910 square miles and borders several US states, including Ohio, Pennsylvania, Michigan, and New York. It is the shallowest Great Lake, which helps it warm faster than the others.Experts suggest this warmer water makes Erie the most biologically productive of the Great Lakes. Millions of migratory birds travel through the region every year, while rare species continue to survive on islands within the lake. Freshwater jellyfish and freshwater sponges have also reportedly been found in its waters, which surprises many visitors.Lake Michigan covers around 22,300 square miles, making it nearly 15 times the area of Rhode Island. It is also the only Great Lake located entirely within the United States.Major cities such as Chicago and Milwaukee sit along its shoreline. The lake is known for its huge freshwater dunes and long beaches, especially around Indiana Dunes National Park. The eastern shoreline has also developed a reputation for fruit farming because the lake helps moderate temperatures year-round.Lake Huron is slightly larger than Lake Michigan, covering about 23,007 square miles. The lake contains the famous Georgian Bay region and thousands of islands scattered across its waters.Experts estimate that nearly 1,000 shipwrecks rest beneath Lake Huron’s surface, preserved by cold freshwater conditions. The lake is also home to Wasaga Beach, reportedly the world’s longest freshwater beach.Large forested areas around the lake continue to support diverse wildlife and relatively undeveloped shorelines.Lake Superior is the largest freshwater lake in the world by surface area, spanning an enormous 31,700 square miles. It borders Ontario, Minnesota, Michigan, and Wisconsin.The lake is deeper and colder than the other Great Lakes, with some parts reaching depths of more than 1,300 feet. Much of the surrounding coastline remains wild and sparsely populated, helping preserve habitats for moose, wolves, lynx, and woodland caribou.Standing beside Lake Superior reportedly feels more like facing an ocean than a lake. The horizon can seem endless, especially during rough weather. It is difficult to fully grasp its scale until seeing it in person.
Article Summary: Six lakes in or partly connected to the US are larger than Rhode Island. Five belong to the Great Lakes system, while the sixth sits along the US-Canada border and contains thousands of islands. Their size, wildlife, and economic importance make them some of the most remarkable natural features on the continent.

Article Title: Trump China Visit: 'US, China should be partners not rivals': Key takeaways from Trump and Xi's much-watched summit
Article Text:
Xi Jinping and Donald Trump (R)

Poll Do you think strong diplomatic talks can help reduce global tensions? Yes, dialogue is very important No, actions matter more than talks







US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping on Thursday struck a warm and optimistic tone during their high-stakes meeting at the Great Hall of the People on Thursday, with both leaders stressing cooperation over confrontation despite tensions over trade, technology and regional security.Xi rolled out red carpet and put up a ceremonial military guard of honour for his American counterpart Donald Trump.The meeting marked Trump’s first visit to China during his second stint of presidency and drew global attention as both countries attempted to reset ties between the world’s two largest economies.Opening the talks, Trump praised the reception given to him and the US delegation, calling it “an honour like few have ever seen before.”"Well, President Xi, I want to thank you very much. First of all, that was an honour like few have ever seen before," Trump said.Trump also praised the Chinese military and said he was deeply impressed by the children who took part in the welcome ceremony."I think I was particularly impressed by those children. They were happy. They were beautiful. The military is obvious. It couldn't be better. But those children were amazing, and they represent so much," he said.Xi, in his opening remarks, said the entire world was closely watching the meeting and framed the talks as a defining moment for future global stability."Welcome back to China after 9 years. The whole world is watching our meeting. Currently, a transformation not seen in a century is accelerating across the globe, and the international situation is fluid and turbulent. The world has come to a new crossroads. Can China and the United States overcome the Thucydides trap and create a new paradigm of major country relations?..."Xi also called for stronger cooperation between Beijing and Washington and warned against rivalry between the two powers.He said, "Can we meet global challenges together and provide more stability for the world? Can we, in the interests of the well-being of our two peoples and the future of humanity, build a brighter future together for our bilateral relations? These are the questions vital to history, to the world, and to the people. They are the questions of our times that you and I need to answer as leaders of major countries... I always believe that our two countries have more common interests than differences. Success in one is an opportunity for the other. And a stable bilateral relationship is good for the world. China and the United States both stand to gain from cooperation and lose from confrontation. We should be partners, not rivals. We should help each other succeed and prosper together and find the right way for major countries to get along well with each other in the new era..."The Chinese president further expressed hope that the talks would open a “new chapter” in bilateral relations."Mr. President, I look forward to our discussions on major issues important to our two countries and the world, and working together with you to set the course for and steer the giant ship of China-US relations so as to make 2026 a historic landmark year that opens up a new chapter in China-US relations," Xi added.Trump, meanwhile, highlighted his long personal relationship with Xi and said the two leaders had always managed to resolve differences directly.MAGA supremo said, "You and I have known each other now for a long time. In fact, the longest relationship of our two countries that any President and President has had. And that's, to me, an honour. We've had a fantastic relationship. We've gotten along. When there were difficulties, we worked it out. I would call you, and you would call me. Whenever we had a problem, we worked it out very quickly and we're going to have a fantastic future together. .."Praising Xi’s leadership, Trump added, "Such respect for China, the job you've done. You're a great leader. I say it to everybody."Trump also pointed to the presence of top American business leaders in his delegation and said they were eager to expand trade ties with China."We have the greatest businessmen, the biggest and I guess the best in the world. We have amazing people and they're all with me," he said.Calling the summit potentially historic, Trump said, "There are those that say this is maybe the biggest summit ever. They can never remember anything like it."Concluding his remarks, Trump said, "It's an honour to be your friend and the relationship between China and the USA is going to be better than ever before."Earlier, Trump was formally welcomed by Xi at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing. The two leaders shared a warm handshake before Trump met members of Xi’s cabinet ahead of the closed-door discussions.
Article Summary: Xi rolled out red carpet and put up a ceremonial military guard of honour for his American counterpart Donald Trump. The meeting marked Trump’s first visit to China during his second stint of presidency and drew global attention as both countries attempted to reset ties between the world's two largest economies.

Article Title: Quote of the day by David Lloyd George: “Don't be afraid to take a big step if one is indicated. You can't cross a chasm in two small jumps.”
Article Text:
David Lloyd George (Image: Wikipedia)

Quote of the day by David Lloyd George



“Don't be afraid to take a big step if one is indicated. You can't cross a chasm in two small jumps”



Who was David Lloyd George



What is the meaning of the quote by David Lloyd George



Why the quote feels strangely modern



Why people delay big decisions for so long



How to apply this quote in daily life



The internet loves this quote for a reason



Why this quote will probably never disappear









Almost everyone has had a moment when they knew a small change was no longer enough.A person keeps talking about leaving a miserable job but never actually resigns. Someone spends years saying they want to move abroad, start a business, lose weight, write a book, or end a relationship that clearly stopped working long ago. The plans stay alive in conversations, notes apps, and late-night thoughts. Real action never fully arrives.That is probably why this old quote from David Lloyd George still spreads across the internet more than a century later.It does not sound soft or comforting. There is no complicated philosophy hiding inside it either. The line works because the image feels instantly real. A giant gap cannot be crossed halfway. Tiny jumps might feel safer, but they still leave a person stuck in the same place.And honestly, modern life seems full of people standing at the edge of those kinds of “chasms.”David Lloyd George was one of Britain’s most famous political leaders and served as Prime Minister during the First World War. Born in 1863, he became known for his fiery speeches, quick wit, and willingness to take political risks that many others avoided.He was raised in Wales and came from a fairly modest background compared to many politicians of his era. That probably shaped some of his political views later. Lloyd George pushed welfare reforms, pensions, and labour protections at a time when Britain was changing rapidly because of industrialisation and social unrest.Historians still argue about parts of his legacy. Some viewed him as brilliant and fearless. Others thought he was too unpredictable or overly ambitious. Either way, he was rarely described as cautious.That personality shows up very clearly in this quote.The quote is really about moments where hesitation stops helping.Lloyd George compares difficult decisions to standing in front of a huge chasm. If the gap is small, careful little steps might work. A massive gap is different. Eventually, a person has to commit fully and leap across it.That idea applies to far more situations than politics.Someone may spend years trying to “slowly” leave a career they hate while becoming more exhausted every year. Another person may know their business is failing, but keeps making tiny adjustments instead of confronting reality directly. People do this emotionally, too. Difficult conversations get delayed. Big choices get postponed. Life stays stuck in limbo.The strange thing is that small jumps often feel productive at first. They create the feeling of movement without requiring real risk.But sometimes nothing actually changes.That seems to be the uncomfortable truth sitting underneath Lloyd George’s words.The quote is old, but it sounds like something written for the internet age.People today live in constant uncertainty. Careers change quickly. Entire industries appear and disappear within a decade. Someone can spend years studying for a profession only to realise the market has completely shifted.Social media probably adds another layer to it. Every day, people watch others announce major life decisions online. One person moves countries. Another quits corporate work to start a café. Somebody launches a YouTube channel after years of hesitation. There is endless visibility into other people taking risks.That creates a weird mix of inspiration and anxiety.Experts who study workplace trends often point out that younger generations now switch careers far more often than previous ones did. The idea of staying in one predictable role for forty years no longer feels realistic for many people.In that kind of world, Lloyd George’s quote suddenly feels very current again.Fear obviously plays a role.Still, it is not always fear of failure. Sometimes people fear embarrassment more than failure itself. Failing privately is one thing. Failing publicly in front of friends, relatives, or social media followers feels much worse.That probably explains why so many people remain stuck in situations they already know are not working.A familiar problem can feel safer than an uncertain future.Psychologists have talked for years about how human beings naturally prefer predictability, even when the predictable situation is making them unhappy. The brain likes routine because routine feels controllable. Big decisions break that sense of control completely.And large life changes rarely arrive looking neat or organised.People quitting jobs usually feel terrified beforehand. Entrepreneurs often admit they had no idea whether things would succeed. Even successful people, later describing their “big leap” moments, often confess they were panicking internally at the time.The confidence usually comes later. That part gets edited out of motivational stories.The quote does not mean people should make reckless decisions overnight. Lloyd George was not saying every dramatic leap automatically leads somewhere good.The important phrase is “if one is indicated.”In other words, when the situation has already become obvious.A student preparing for competitive exams may eventually realise that inconsistent effort is not enough anymore. Somebody trying to improve their health may understand that temporary motivation will not solve long-term habits. A person trapped in an unhappy environment may recognise that endless waiting is quietly making things worse.The quote becomes useful in moments like that.It asks a difficult question: are small adjustments genuinely helping, or are they just delaying necessary action?That is probably why people keep sharing the line online. Most readers can immediately connect it to something personal in their own lives.Some quotes disappear after a few years because they sound dated or overly dramatic. This one survived because the metaphor is simple enough for almost anybody to understand immediately.A giant gap. Two tiny jumps.Failure becomes obvious before the sentence even ends.Social media also rewards quotes that feel visually strong. Lloyd George’s line almost creates a movie scene in the reader’s head. A person standing near the edge of something enormous, knowing that hesitation will not solve the problem.That image sticks.There is another reason the quote keeps circulating, too. Modern culture constantly encourages people to dream bigger while simultaneously making them terrified of mistakes. That contradiction creates paralysis. People want change, but also want guarantees before taking action.Life rarely gives those guarantees.Some sayings survive because they describe permanent parts of human behaviour.Fear of uncertainty is one of them.Every generation faces moments where the safe option slowly stops working. The details change with time. One era deals with industrial change, another with artificial intelligence, social media pressure, or unstable job markets. The emotional experience underneath remains surprisingly similar.People still hesitate before major decisions. They still wait for perfect timing. They still hope tiny adjustments will solve situations that probably require something much bigger.That is what makes Lloyd George’s quote feel timeless. It does not promise success or pretend that bravery is easy. It simply points out something most people already know deep down.Some gaps in life cannot be crossed halfway.
Article Summary: David Lloyd George was one of Britain’s most famous political leaders. He became known for his fiery speeches, quick wit, and willingness to take risks. Lloyd George compares difficult decisions to standing in front of a huge chasm. The quote is really about moments where hesitation stops helping.

Article Title: Alex Batty: UK’s kidnapped teen Alex Batty says life with mother on the run became unbearable
Article Text:
Alex Batty, missing for six years since age 11, has spoken out about his ordeal in a new documentary. He endured a nomadic life across Spain, Morocco, and France, kept from school. Found alone in the French Pyrenees, Alex has since been reunited with his grandmother. A police investigation has concluded with no prosecution.







Alex Batty was 11 years old when he left the UK with his mother and grandfather for what was supposed to be a holiday to Marbella. He did not come home for six years. Now 20, he is speaking publicly about what that time was really like in a new BBC documentary titled Kidnapped By My Mum.According to BBC, coverage of the documentary Alex described a nomadic life spent moving between communes and rural areas across Spain, Morocco and south-west France. He was kept out of school throughout those years. He said he gradually grew unhappy with the lifestyle and spoke about a moment that stayed with him: sitting at a cafe near a school and hearing the bell ring as children came out. "I cried my eyes out because you know, I'd had enough," he said.Alex disappeared in October 2017 after travelling abroad with his mother Melanie Batty and his grandfather David Batty. His grandmother Susan Caruana, who was his legal guardian back in Greater Manchester, reported him missing when they did not return.In December 2023 Alex walked away from a remote area in the French Pyrenees on his own. A delivery driver found him walking alone near Toulouse in the early hours and took him to the police.Alex identified himself and was subsequently reunited with his grandmother in the UK.Assistant Chief Constable Chris Sykes of Greater Manchester Police said at the time: "I think I speak on behalf of the whole of Greater Manchester Police when I say we were relieved and overjoyed to receive news from the French Authorities that they believe Alex Batty had been located safe and well. "Greater Manchester Police launched an investigation into the disappearance after Alex returned. In January 2025 they confirmed the criminal investigation had been closed after concluding there was no realistic chance of prosecution and that the family did not support criminal charges.Melanie Batty's current whereabouts are not publicly known. She declined to respond to allegations made in the documentary.
Article Summary: Alex Batty was 11 years old when he left the UK with his mother and grandfather for what was supposed to be a holiday to Marbella. He endured a nomadic life across Spain, Morocco, and France, kept from school. Found alone in the French Pyrenees, Alex has since been reunited with his grandmother. A police investigation has concluded with no prosecution.

Article Title: ‘Whether Starmer stays or goes...’: Larry the Cat’s viral post steals spotlight amid UK political drama
Article Text:
Larry the Cat







As new political chaos hit UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer amid growing discussion around his leadership, many social media users appeared to rally behind one familiar Downing Street figure who has outlasted governments, cabinet reshuffles and political turmoil for years, Larry the Cat.The long-serving Downing Street mouser’s X post soon went viral online. The post read: “Whether Starmer stays or goes we can all agree that me being fed is the most important thing.”Larry, the grey-and-white tabby who serves as Chief Mouser at 10 Downing Street, has become one of Britain’s most recognisable political mascots over the years.Originally rescued from Battersea Dogs & Cats Home, Larry moved into Downing Street in 2011 and has since served under six British prime ministers.Now regarded as something of a permanent resident of British politics, the cat frequently appears in photographs with world leaders, journalists and government officials outside the prime minister’s residence.The viral post quickly drew thousands of reactions online, with many users joking that Larry had become the only stable figure left in Westminster politics.“prime minsters come and go but larry is for ever,” one user wrote.Another commented: “Can you just take over and have done with it please. Larry for PM.”Others reflected on the number of political leaders Larry has seen during his years at Downing Street.“I can't believe how many top level servants you have got through. I bet you were rehomed hoping for some stability in your life. Hope there is a requirements list when there are changeovers eg minimum amount of Dreamies per day,” another person posted.Having completed 15 years at Downing Street earlier this year, Larry remains a familiar presence outside Number 10 and continues to occupy a unique place in British political culture.
Article Summary: Long-serving Downing Street mouser’s X post soon went viral online. The post read: “Whether Starmer stays or goes we can all agree that me being fed is the most important thing.” Larry, the grey-and-white tabby who serves as Chief Mouser at 10 Downing Street, has become one of Britain's most recognisable political mascots.

Article Title: Uk Women Lottery Prize: ‘She’s luckier than me’: UK woman wins £333,000 lottery prize after husband secretly put her namec
Article Text: An 80-year-old woman from Cheshire, UK, has won £333,333 in the People’s Postcode Lottery after her husband secretly registered the ticket in her name because he believed she brought better luck.Nancy Barkley, from Crewe, landed the six-figure payout as part of a £1 million shared prize awarded across her neighbourhood.Her husband Colin Barkley, 69, said he deliberately signed up using his wife’s details because he was convinced she was the lucky one in the family.“I told her she was the lucky one. Looks like I was right,” he said.The couple now plan to leave their rented accommodation and finally buy a home of their own after years of living modestly.“We can buy the bungalow that we've always wanted,” Nancy said.“We're not flashy people. We don't drink and we're happy with simple things.”The retired factory worker described the win as life-changing and admitted she initially thought the prize amount would be much smaller.“I never expected anything like that amount,” she said.“I thought £1,000... anything over that is a bonus. This is a blooming big bonus.”Nancy also reflected on her late brother David Whitby, who was attacked during the 1963 Great Train Robbery carried out by one of Britain’s most notorious criminal gangs, the BBC reported.She said the family tragedy had remained part of her life for decades and wondered whether her brother was somehow watching over her.“Everyone all knew the robbers' names but now people remember my brother David as well,” she said.“And maybe he's looking down on us today.”The grandmother-of-two previously worked as a cutter at Chester Barrie, a clothing manufacturer known for producing suits worn by royals, footballers and Formula One figures.The couple recently returned to Crewe after retiring to Wales, saying worsening health issues brought them closer to relatives.Nancy was diagnosed with breast cancer two years ago and later suffered serious complications following treatment.“At one point I went a whole month hardly eating or drinking,” she said.“I ended up in hospital on drips and intravenous antibiotics.”She said her health has since improved and the family now hopes to enjoy quieter pleasures, including lunches out and short trips away.
Article Summary: Nancy Barkley, from Crewe, landed the six-figure payout as part of a £1 million shared prize awarded across her neighbourhood. Her husband Colin Barkley, 69, said he deliberately signed up using his wife’s details because he was convinced she was the lucky one in the family.
